name: Capability fragmentation across agent surfaces
instance_fixed: Bot said 'I can't create channels' despite having create-channels.js; bot ignored its own MCP-sent messages
siblings_detail: create-channels.js not wired as bot tool (fixed: added create_channel governance tool)
message.author.bot blanket rejection blocked self-testing (fixed: [DEMERZEL-TEST] bypass)
#clarity-lab channel not in shouldRespond() or detectPersona() routing (fixed: added)
detection_added: capability-unity-policy.yaml detection section: staleness scan for scripts/ not exposed as tools, behavioral tests per commit, weekly surface parity audit
prevention_added: capability-unity-policy.yaml prevention section: pre-merge checklist (4 items) and capability manifest requirement
system_change: New policy: capability-unity-policy.yaml — prevents the entire class of surface fragmentation. New behavioral tests: capability-unity-cases.md (8 test cases). Governance tools added to bot alongside music tools.
root_cause: Capabilities were added to whichever surface was convenient (script for batch, bot for chat, MCP for Claude Code) with no cross-surface registration requirement
class_of_problem: Agent self-awareness gap — multiple surfaces of the same agent don't know about each other's capabilities
amdahls_law_insight: Each surface hop is a serial bottleneck. Unified capability registration eliminates the hop.
